user_message: I need you to process this support ticket from Alpine Ski House. There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 14:20 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 17:16 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 17:10 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-L trap (Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-L failure mode (Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-L -- Stakeholder-map refresh as separate proposal vs inline verification
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
